title: circaea lutetiana var. intermedia
intro: Circaea lutetiana var. intermedia, also known as the intermediate wood sorrel, is a subspecies of the wood sorrel plant, a perennial herb native to Europe and Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Circaea lutetiana var. intermedia include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues, such as diarrhea and stomach ulcers. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects.
